doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document grossly misrepresents Illinois eviction law by falsely claiming tenants must vacate without any court process, which contradicts the Illinois Forcible Entry and Detainer Act and is something HUD would never publish.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 99
REASON: This document fundamentally misrepresents Illinois tenant law — under the Illinois Forcible Entry and Detainer Act, a 5-day notice does NOT require tenants to vacate without court proceedings; landlords must file an eviction lawsuit and obtain a court order before a tenant is legally required to leave, and tenants absolutely have the right to a court hearing. HUD would never issue guidance telling tenants they have no right to legal counsel or a court hearing, and the document number, co-issuance format, phone number, and future effective date are all unverifiable fabrications designed to trick tenants into waiving fundamental legal rights.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 99
REASON: The document grossly misrepresents Illinois eviction law by falsely claiming tenants must vacate without any court process, which contradicts the Illinois Forcible Entry and Detainer Act and is something HUD would never publish.
